user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of credentialing updates I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your malpractice coverage record is updated with your new carrier, new policy number, coverage limits, and policy dates, and you receive a case ID confirming the update.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want to request a provisional 90-day license extension for your state license, but if you are told a new request cannot be submitted because an extension request is already pending or was already submitted on your behalf, ask one follow-up question for clarification and then accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing provider verification only after the agent asks for authentication details. Provide your NPI, home facility code, and 4-digit PIN when requested. Do not volunteer additional identifiers before being asked.
2. After authentication, say you have two items to handle: first your malpractice coverage update, and second a license extension question. Keep this high level until the agent asks for details.

3. For the malpractice update intent, wait for the agent to review your current malpractice record first. When asked for the new details, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date exactly as listed in information_required.

4. If the agent reads back any malpractice identifiers, policy numbers, limits, or dates, confirm them only if they exactly match information_required. If any value is read back incorrectly, correct only the incorrect value and nothing else.
5. When the agent summarizes the malpractice update before submitting, explicitly confirm the submission. Stay on the call until the malpractice update is completed and a case ID is provided.

6. After the malpractice item is resolved, raise the second intent: say you also need a provisional license extension because your license is nearing expiration. Wait for the agent to ask for the license details, then provide the state license number, extension type, and extension days exactly as listed in information_required.

7. If the agent says an extension request is already pending or was already submitted by your department, ask exactly one follow-up question to clarify the status, such as asking whether that means you do not need to submit anything else right now. After the agent answers, accept the denial and do not push further.
8. If the agent instead asks for a supervising physician NPI, say this is a provisional extension, so you are requesting the provisional type. Do not provide any supervising physician information.
9. At the end, make sure you have the malpractice update case ID and a clear explanation of the license-extension outcome before ending the call.

Guiding behavioral directions: Do not volunteer specific policy, license, or date details until the agent asks for them. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant question, answer only with the exact information from information_required if available; otherwise say you only have the details already provided in this call. Accept straightforward yes/no confirmations with a simple yes or no. Do not invent missing records, alternate carriers, alternate dates, or additional requests.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your malpractice coverage update, and you have received a clear explanation that a new provisional license extension request cannot be submitted because one is already pending or was already submitted on your behalf. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether the license extension is provisional or supervised, choose provisional.
If asked for an extension duration, choose 90 days.
If asked for a supervising physician NPI, say none applies because this is a provisional extension request.
If the agent says the extension is already pending, do not try to submit a duplicate request.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a recently credentialed provider who switched malpractice carriers and also need help with an expiring license. You want to update your malpractice coverage record and ask for a provisional 90-day license extension, but you may be told the malpractice record was never initialized and that a license extension has already been submitted.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of credentialing-related things I need help with.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want to update your malpractice coverage record, but if you are told this is not possible because no malpractice record was ever initialized, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
You want to request a provisional 90-day license extension, but if you are told this is not possible because an extension has already been submitted, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin with provider verification only. When asked, provide your NPI, facility code, and 4-digit PIN. Do not volunteer other identifiers until the agent asks.

2. After verification, briefly state that you have two items: a malpractice coverage update and a license extension question. Let the agent choose which one to handle first; do not insist on an order.

3. For the malpractice intent, do not volunteer policy details until asked. When the agent asks, provide your new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date exactly as listed in information_required.

4. If the agent says they cannot update the malpractice coverage because no malpractice record exists or credentialing never initialized it, ask exactly one follow-up question: whether there is any way to create or activate the record from this call. If the answer is still no, accept the denial and move on.

5. For the license-extension intent, do not volunteer license details until asked. When the agent asks, provide your state license number, requested extension type, requested extension length, and license expiration date exactly as listed in information_required.

6. If the agent says they cannot submit a new extension because one has already been submitted or is already pending, ask exactly one follow-up question: whether they can at least confirm that an extension is already on file. If the answer is still that no new action can be taken, accept the denial.

7. If the agent reads back any identifier, policy number, coverage amount, or license number, confirm it if it matches information_required. If it does not match, correct only the mismatched value and nothing else.

8. Do not volunteer extra background, speculate about internal processes, or invent missing facts. If the agent asks an unexpected question that is not covered by information_required, say you do not have additional information beyond what you already provided.
9. Before ending the call, make sure both intents have been addressed with a clear outcome. If both are denied, accept the explanations and wrap up politely.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a clear explanation that your malpractice coverage record cannot be updated because no malpractice record was initialized, and a clear explanation that no new license extension can be submitted because an extension was already submitted. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether the license extension should be provisional or supervised, choose provisional.
If asked for the extension duration, choose 90 days.
If the agent asks for a supervising physician NPI, say that this is a provisional extension and you are not requesting a supervised extension.
If the agent asks whether your new malpractice coverage is below the organizational minimum, say no.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are returning from personal leave and moving into a new role at a different facility in your hospital system. You need to transfer your DEA registration to the new site so you can prescribe controlled substances there, and you also need your suspended clinical privileges reactivated so you can resume clinical work.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR credentialing things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your DEA registration transfer to the new facility is completed and you receive a case ID or other explicit confirmation identifier for the transfer.

Your suspended clinical privileges are reactivated, your competency review is scheduled, your EHR access restoration choice is applied, and you receive a case ID or other explicit confirmation identifier for the privilege reactivation.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by briefly saying you have two HR/credentialing issues to handle, but do not volunteer details until the agent asks. Complete whichever authentication steps the agent requests. Because this call involves a DEA transfer and privilege reactivation, provide provider verification details when asked, then complete OTP when asked. If the agent asks to confirm the last four digits of the phone number on file before reading the OTP, confirm them only if they match the last four digits of your phone_number in information_required.

2. First intent: DEA transfer. When the agent asks what you need first, say you need to transfer your DEA registration to your new facility. Do not volunteer the DEA number, new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, or effective date until asked. When asked, provide exactly the values from information_required. If the agent reads back the DEA number, current facility, or new facility details, confirm them if correct. If the agent asks for the state as a full name or abbreviation, provide the value from information_required; if they want the abbreviation specifically, use the 2-letter code already provided there.

3. If the agent asks you to confirm the DEA transfer details before submission, explicitly confirm. Stay on the call until the transfer is actually completed and a case ID or explicit confirmation identifier is given. If the agent says the PDMP or downstream notification has been sent, acknowledge briefly and continue.

4. Second intent: privilege reactivation. After the DEA transfer is handled, say you also need your clinical privileges reactivated after returning from personal leave. Do not volunteer your NPI again unless asked. When asked, provide the clearance code, leave type on record, and confirm exactly which suspended privileges you want reactivated. If asked which privileges to reactivate, request exactly the privilege_codes listed in information_required and no others.

5. For the required competency review appointment, first state your preferred date and preferred time from information_required. If that exact slot is available, accept it. If that time is not available but other slots are available on the same preferred date, accept the earliest available time on that same date. If no slots are available on the preferred date, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future alternative date presented by the agent. If multiple alternative dates are offered, choose the earliest date; if multiple times are offered on that date, choose the earliest time.

6. If the agent asks what level of EHR access should be restored, choose exactly the access_change_type in information_required. If the agent summarizes the privilege reactivation request, appointment, leave type, selected privileges, or EHR access, confirm only if all items match the values in information_required.

7. Stay on the call until the privilege reactivation is actually completed and you receive a case ID or explicit confirmation identifier, and the competency review appointment date and time are confirmed back to you.
8. At the end, if both intents have been completed with confirmation identifiers and the appointment is scheduled, thank the agent briefly and end the call.

Guiding behavior: Never volunteer more information than the agent has asked for. Answer only the specific question asked using the exact value from information_required. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarifying question, answer with the corresponding information from information_required if it exists; otherwise say you only need help with the defined requests. For any read-back of IDs, dates, codes, facility codes, or privilege codes, confirm only if every character and date matches exactly.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID or explicit confirmation identifier for the DEA registration transfer to the new facility, and you have received a case ID or explicit confirmation identifier for the clinical privilege reactivation with the competency review appointment scheduled and EHR access restoration confirmed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked which privileges to reactivate, request all of the privilege codes listed in information_required.
If asked what type of leave you were on, choose the leave_type_on_record from information_required exactly.
If asked what EHR access should be restored, choose the access_change_type from information_required exactly.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are returning from personal leave and moving to a new facility. You need to transfer your DEA registration to the new location, and you also want to try to reactivate your suspended clinical privileges, even though you may be told that cannot be completed because your occupational health clearance record is outdated or does not match.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR items I need help with as I come back from leave.

must_have_criteria: Your DEA registration transfer to the new facility is completed and you receive a case ID confirming the transfer.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want your suspended clinical privileges reactivated, but if you are told this is not possible because your occupational health clearance cannot be validated or does not match current records, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by saying you have a couple of HR items related to returning from leave, but do not volunteer detailed identifiers until asked.

2. Complete authentication in the order the agent requests. Because this call includes a DEA transfer and a privilege matter, provide provider verification details when asked, then complete OTP when asked. If the agent also asks for employee-verification-style details later, provide them without arguing.

3. For the first intent, state that you need to transfer your DEA registration to a new facility. Wait for the agent to ask for each detail, then provide your DEA number, new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, and effective date exactly as listed in information_required.
4. If the agent reads back any identifier or date for the DEA transfer, confirm it only if it matches exactly. If any value is read back incorrectly, correct only the incorrect value.
5. If the agent asks you to confirm the DEA transfer before submission, say yes. Stay on the call until the transfer is completed and you are given the case ID.

6. After the DEA transfer is handled, raise the second intent by saying you also need your clinical privileges reactivated after leave. Do not volunteer all related details at once; wait for the agent to ask.

7. For the privilege request, provide your clearance code, NPI if asked again, leave type on record, the privilege codes you want reactivated, and the EHR access restoration type exactly as listed in information_required. If asked which privileges to reactivate, request exactly the listed privilege codes and no others.

8. If the agent asks for a preferred competency review appointment, first request 2026-09-17 at 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM on 2026-09-17 is unavailable, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-09-17. If no slots are available on 2026-09-17, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date.

9. If the agent says the privilege reactivation cannot be completed because the occupational health clearance is outdated, from before the leave, or does not match current records, ask one follow-up question: whether anything can be done over the phone today to fix it. After that single follow-up, accept the denial.
10. At the end, confirm the completed DEA transfer case ID and the final status of the privilege reactivation request, then end the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are returning from personal leave and moving your work to a new facility, and you want HR help with two things in one call: trying to transfer your DEA registration and trying to reactivate your suspended clinical privileges, while accepting that either or both may be denied because your DEA information may not match the record and your occupational health clearance is outdated.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of provider HR items I need help with today.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want your DEA registration transferred to the new facility, but if you are told this is not possible because the DEA number cannot be verified or the transfer cannot be processed, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.

You want your suspended clinical privileges reactivated, but if you are told this is not possible because your occupational health clearance is outdated or you are otherwise ineligible, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by saying you have a couple of provider HR items to handle. Do not volunteer detailed identifiers until the agent asks for them.

2. Complete authentication in the order requested. For provider verification, provide your NPI, home facility code, and PIN only when asked. If OTP is initiated, first confirm the last four digits of the phone number on file only if they match your phone_number, then provide the OTP code only when asked.

3. For the first intent, say you want to transfer your DEA registration to a new facility. If asked for the DEA details, provide the DEA number, new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, and effective date. If the agent reads back the DEA number or other identifiers, confirm them exactly if they match the information_required values.

4. If the agent says the DEA transfer cannot be completed because the DEA number does not match the record, cannot be found, or is otherwise invalid, ask exactly one follow-up question: ask whether there is any way to verify the correct DEA record or proceed another way. After the agent answers that one follow-up, accept the denial and move on to the privilege issue.

5. For the second intent, say you also need your suspended clinical privileges reactivated because you are returning from personal leave. Do not volunteer all details at once; wait for the agent to ask for your NPI, clearance code, leave type, privilege selections, and access level.

6. If asked which privileges to reactivate, request exactly the privilege codes listed in information_required and no others. If the agent asks you to confirm one by one, confirm each one individually. If asked what type of EHR access to restore, choose the access_change_type from information_required.

7. If the agent asks for a preferred competency review appointment date and time, state your preferred_date and preferred_time first. If that exact slot is unavailable, accept the earliest offered slot on the same date. If no slots are available on that date, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date presented by the agent.

8. If the agent says the privilege reactivation cannot be completed because the occupational health clearance is outdated, ask exactly one follow-up question: ask whether a current clearance is required before reactivation can proceed. After the agent answers that one follow-up, accept the denial.

9. For any read-back of names, codes, dates, or identifiers, simply confirm if correct. If the agent misreads a value, correct only the specific incorrect field using the exact value from information_required.

10. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions, answer only with information from information_required. If the question asks for something not provided there, say you do not have any additional information.
11. Once both intents have been addressed, listen for the final summary. If the outcomes are clearly explained for both requests, acknowledge briefly and end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a clear final outcome for both intents: a clear explanation that the DEA transfer cannot be completed and a clear explanation that the privilege reactivation cannot be completed, including any case or reference numbers if the agent generated them for attempted processing or review. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, says the request cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, says you must visit HR in person, or says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.

Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.

3. Handle the first intent first: malpractice coverage update. After the agent retrieves and reviews your current malpractice record, confirm that you need to update it for a new carrier covering your new state. 8. If the agent reads back the DEA number, new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, or effective date, confirm only if they exactly match information_required. If not, correct only the mistaken field and wait for the corrected read-back before approving submission.

9. Before ending the call, stay on the line until you have a concrete confirmation for both actions: one case ID for the malpractice update and one case ID for the DEA transfer. A verbal promise to submit later is not enough.

10. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions that are not covered elsewhere, answer only with facts already present in information_required. negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing provider verification only when asked. Provide your NPI, home facility code, and 4-digit PIN. Do not volunteer other details before the agent asks.
2. After verification, say you need two things handled: first your malpractice coverage update, then your DEA transfer request.

3. For the malpractice update intent, wait for the agent to retrieve and review your current malpractice record. If the agent reads back the existing record, simply confirm whether it sounds like the current record on file.

4. Only when asked for the new malpractice details, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date exactly as listed in information_required.

5. If the agent reads back the malpractice carrier, policy number, or coverage limits, listen for accuracy and answer 'yes' only if the values exactly match information_required; otherwise correct only the specific field that was read back incorrectly.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a provider relocating to another state and you want to take care of two credentialing-related items: update your malpractice coverage and transfer your DEA registration, but you will accept being told these cannot be completed if the records cannot be found.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of provider record updates I need help with.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want to update your malpractice coverage record, but if you are told this is not possible because no malpractice record exists on file, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
You want to transfer your DEA registration, but if you are told this is not possible because the DEA number does not match any record on file, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing provider verification only when asked. Provide your NPI, home facility code, and PIN. If the agent proceeds to DEA transfer handling, complete OTP verification when asked using the OTP code. Do not volunteer any verification details before the agent asks.

2. Give a brief high-level overview only after verification starts or completes: say you need help with a malpractice coverage update and a DEA transfer related to your move. Do not provide detailed policy or DEA information until the agent asks for each item.

3. First intent — malpractice coverage update: if asked, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date. If the agent says no malpractice record exists or that credentialing never initialized the record, ask exactly one follow-up question asking whether there is any way to complete or open it by phone today. If the agent still says it cannot be done, accept the denial and move on.

4. Second intent — DEA transfer: if asked, provide the DEA number, new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, and effective date. If OTP is requested for the DEA portion, provide the OTP code when asked. If the agent says the DEA number does not match any record on file, ask exactly one follow-up question asking whether they can re-check the number or find it another way. If the agent still says it cannot be done, accept the denial and move on.

5. If the agent reads back any identifier, code, policy number, DEA number, facility code, or date, confirm it only if it exactly matches the value in information_required. If it does not match, correct only the specific wrong value.

6. Do not volunteer extra context, guesses, alternate numbers, or substitute records. If the agent asks for information not present in information_required, say you only have the details already provided and ask them to continue with that.
7. Wrap up only after both intents have been addressed with a clear outcome. If both are denied, acknowledge the explanations politely and end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a clear explanation that your malpractice coverage update cannot be completed because no malpractice record exists on file, and a clear explanation that your DEA transfer cannot be completed because the DEA number does not match any record on file. End the call.

decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

If asked to confirm submission, say yes. Stay on the call until the registration is completed and a registration ID is given.

7. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarification questions, answer only with information contained in information_required. If the agent asks for information not defined there, say you do not have any additional details beyond what you already provided.
8. At the end, confirm both reference numbers only if they are read back to you, then end the call.

2. After verification, say you have two things to handle today: a shift swap and an on-call availability registration. Do not give the detailed dates or colleague information until the agent asks for them.

3. For the first intent, the shift swap: when asked for the shift details, provide the shift date 2026-09-19, shift start time 01:00 AM, shift end time 01:00 PM, and colleague employee ID. If the agent asks for the, instead, provide it exactly as listed in information_required.

4. If the agent says the shift cannot be swapped because it is within 24 hours, ask exactly one follow-up question: ask whether there is any exception or workaround for this shift. After the agent answers that follow-up, accept the denial and do not argue further.

5. After the shift swap outcome is clear, move to the second intent, on-call registration. When asked, provide the unit code, the availability start date, the availability end date, the on-call tier, and say there are no blackout dates.

6. If the agent says you are not eligible to register for on-call because you are currently on approved leave, ask exactly one follow-up question: ask whether the registration can be entered now for the future window once your leave status changes. After the agent answers that follow-up, accept the denial and do not argue further.

7. If the agent asks you to confirm a read-back of any ID, date, or code, simply confirm if it matches the information_required values. If it does not match, correct only the specific incorrect value.

8. Do not volunteer extra details beyond what the agent asks for. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant question that is answerable from information_required, answer it exactly using that data. If the question asks for information not provided here, say you do not have that information available.
9. Once both intents have been addressed and both denials have been clearly explained, briefly acknowledge the explanation and end the call.

negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin with provider authentication only when asked. Provide your NPI, home facility code, and 4-digit PIN. user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need to file FMLA leave so you can care for your parent during an extended serious illness, and you also want to request a few general PTO days in the week before the leave starts to handle initial appointments and family logistics.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of leave requests I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your FMLA leave case for caring for your parent is opened successfully, and you receive the FMLA case ID.
Your PTO request for the dates before your FMLA leave is submitted successfully, and you receive the PTO case ID with the deducted days confirmed.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by giving only a high-level overview that you have two leave-related requests. Do not volunteer dates, leave category, or PTO type until the agent asks.

2. Complete authentication in the required order for the sensitive leave request: provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked, confirm the last four digits of the phone number on file if asked, and then provide the OTP code when asked. If the OTP is said to be incorrect, retry the same OTP code once only if prompted for a second attempt.

3. Handle the first intent first: when the agent asks about the leave request, state that you need FMLA to care for your parent with a serious illness. Provide the leave category only if the agent asks for the formal category.

4. For the FMLA details, provide the leave start date and leave end date only when asked. Use the dates from information_required exactly. Do not offer extra medical details beyond that it is for caring for your parent.

5. If the agent asks to schedule the required return-to-work check-in, first request the preferred date 2026-10-21 and preferred time 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM on 2026-10-21 is available, accept it. If 10:00 AM is not available but other slots are available on 2026-10-21, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-10-21. If no slots are available on 2026-10-21, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date on or after 2026-10-21.
6. After the FMLA request is completed, move to the second intent. Say you also want to use PTO for a few days the week before the leave starts. Do not volunteer the PTO type until the agent asks.

7. For the PTO request, if asked whether to use PTO or sick leave, choose PTO. When asked for the dates, provide the exact start and end dates from information_required. If the agent reads back which dates count and how many days will be deducted, simply confirm if the read-back matches the requested range and counted days returned by the agent.

8. If the agent says any requested PTO date is blocked by eligibility rules or blackout dates, ask one follow-up question asking which date caused the issue. Then accept changing or dropping the PTO request only if the agent cannot process the original dates. Do not invent alternate dates unless the agent explicitly asks for a replacement; if asked for a replacement, choose the earliest offered future date range of the same length that ends before the FMLA start date.

9. Before each submission, if the agent summarizes the request, listen and confirm only if the summary matches the information_required values or the deterministic fallback choice already established during the call.
10. End the call only after both intents are resolved with concrete confirmations: a case ID for the FMLA leave, completion of the required check-in scheduling, and a case ID for the PTO request.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your FMLA leave, your return-to-work check-in has been scheduled with a confirmed date and time, and you have received a case ID for your PTO request with the deducted days confirmed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked which family member the FMLA leave is for, say it is for your parent.
If asked whether the pre-leave time off should be general PTO or sick leave, choose PTO.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need to ask HR about filing FMLA leave so you can care for your parent who has a serious illness, and you also want to request a few sick days right before that leave starts, even though you may not qualify yet.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of leave-related things I need help with.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want to file FMLA leave for caregiving for your parent's serious illness, but if you are told you are not eligible because you have not been employed long enough or have insufficient hours, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
You want to request sick leave PTO for the days before the intended caregiving leave, but if you are told you do not have enough sick leave balance, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by giving only a high-level overview that you have leave-related requests. Do not volunteer detailed dates or reasons until the agent asks.

2. Complete authentication when asked: provide your employee ID and date of birth first. Because leave is involved, if the agent initiates OTP, confirm the phone number by giving the last four digits of your phone number and then provide the OTP code when asked.

3. Handle the first intent first: if the agent asks what leave you need, say you want to file FMLA leave to care for your parent. If asked for the leave category, say family_member_serious_illness. If asked for dates, provide the requested FMLA leave start date 2026-08-27 and end date 2026-10-09.

4. If the agent says you are not eligible for FMLA because you have been employed less than 12 months or have not worked 1,250 hours, ask exactly one follow-up question: ask whether there is any way to submit it now or whether you need to wait until you become eligible. After the answer, accept the denial and do not argue further.

5. After the FMLA outcome is clear, move to the second intent: say you also want to request sick leave PTO right before that leave. If asked for dates, provide 2026-08-24 as the start date and 2026-08-26 as the end date. If asked which bank to use, choose sick leave.

6. If the agent reviews your balance and says you do not have enough sick leave available, ask exactly one follow-up question: ask whether any part of those dates can be approved or whether there is another leave option. After the answer, accept the denial and do not create a new request unless the agent directly offers a concrete alternative process.
7. If the agent reads back any identifiers, dates, or leave details, confirm them if they match the information in this goal. If they do not match, correct only the incorrect item and nothing else.
8. Do not volunteer extra personal or medical details about your parent beyond what is needed to identify the leave reason. Only answer the specific question asked.

9. If the agent offers alternatives that do not require you to choose among multiple comparable options, you may accept a clearly stated alternative only if it is explicitly offered by the agent as the next required step; otherwise, stick to the two original requests and accept denial if ineligible.
10. Once both requests have been clearly denied or otherwise resolved, thank the agent, confirm your understanding, and wrap up the call.

user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your payroll correction for the affected shift is submitted using the correct total hours worked, and you receive a payroll correction case ID.
Your upcoming shift swap with your colleague is confirmed, and you receive a shift swap case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by confirming your identity when asked, using only the requested authentication details, and say you have two issues to handle: a payroll problem first and then a shift swap.
2. For the payroll correction intent, wait for the agent to ask which shift is affected. Identify the shift by its date and start time first.

3. For the payroll correction intent, when asked about the problem, state that you worked 12 total hours on that shift but only 10 were logged, and that the correction type is on-call hours. If asked for the corrected hours, give the correct total hours worked, not the difference.

4. For the payroll correction intent, if the agent reads back the shift details, logged hours, correction type, or corrected hours, listen carefully and confirm only if they exactly match the information_required values. If any item is wrong, correct only the incorrect item and do not add new details.
5. After the payroll correction is completed, ask to handle the shift swap. Wait for the agent to ask which upcoming shift you want to swap. Identify the shift by its date and start time first.
6. For the shift swap intent, when asked who you want to swap with, provide your colleague's employee ID only. Do not volunteer assumptions about their certifications; let the agent check that.

7. For the shift swap intent, if the agent confirms the shift details or colleague ID, confirm only if they exactly match the information_required values. If there is any mismatch, correct the mismatch directly. If the agent says the colleague is eligible and asks whether to proceed, say yes.
8. Stay on the call until both actions are completed and you receive a separate case ID for the payroll correction and a separate case ID for the shift swap.
9. Guiding rule: never volunteer specific identifiers or detailed values before the agent asks for them, except for the high-level statement that you have two issues to handle.

10. Guiding rule: if the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarifying question, answer using only the exact information in information_required. If the answer is not present there, say you do not have any additional information beyond what you already provided.

11. Guiding rule: if the agent summarizes either request before submitting it, confirm only when the summary exactly matches the known details; otherwise, provide a correction and wait for a new read-back.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for the payroll correction with the corrected total hours confirmed, and you have received a case ID for the shift swap with the colleague. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked for the corrected payroll hours, provide the total hours actually worked for that shift: 12.0.
If asked for the payroll correction type, choose on_call_hours.
If asked for the affected unit in relation to the shift swap, provide the unit code in information_required.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need to fix missing on-call hours from a previous shift on your timesheet and also ask about swapping your shift that starts tomorrow, even though you may be told that swap cannot be done because it is inside the 24-hour lockout window.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR issues I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your payroll correction for the missing on-call hours is submitted successfully for the specified prior shift, and you receive a case ID.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want to swap your shift that starts tomorrow with your colleague, but if you are told this is not possible because it is within the 24-hour lockout window, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing standard employee authentication only when asked. Provide your employee ID and date of birth, and do not volunteer other details before the agent asks.
2. After authentication, say you have two things to handle: a payroll issue first and then a shift swap question.
3. For the payroll correction intent, wait for the agent to ask which shift or when it occurred. Then provide the payroll correction shift date and start time for the prior shift.

4. If the agent asks for the correction details, provide the correction type and the corrected total hours exactly as listed in information_required. If the agent reads back the shift details or hours, confirm them if they match exactly; otherwise correct only the mismatched field.
5. If the agent asks for confirmation before submitting the payroll correction, explicitly confirm. Stay on the call until the correction is actually submitted and a case ID is given.

6. After the payroll correction is completed, raise the second intent by saying you also want to swap your shift that starts tomorrow with your colleague. Wait for the agent to ask for the shift details and colleague information before providing them.

7. For the shift swap intent, provide the shift date and start time for the tomorrow shift and then provide the colleague employee ID when asked. If the agent asks for the unit, provide the unit code from information_required.

8. If the agent says the shift cannot be swapped because it starts within 24 hours, ask exactly one follow-up question asking whether there is any exception or workaround for the lockout. If the agent still says no, accept the denial and do not argue further.

9. If the agent offers to note the denial reason or explains the policy, acknowledge it briefly and move on. Do not request any alternative scheduling service unless the agent specifically proposes one.

10. Before ending the call, make sure you have the payroll correction case ID and a clear final answer that the shift swap cannot be processed due to the 24-hour lockout window. Then thank the agent and wrap up.
Provide details only in response to direct questions; do not volunteer IDs, dates, or hours earlier than necessary.
If the agent reads back any identifier, date, time, or hours, confirm only if it matches exactly what is in information_required; otherwise provide the corrected value exactly.

If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarifying question, answer using only the information in information_required. If the question asks for information not present there, say you do not have any additional details.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your payroll correction for the missing on-call hours, and you have received a clear explanation that the shift swap cannot be completed because the shift starts within the 24-hour lockout window. End the call.

If asked how many hours should be recorded, give the total correct hours, not the difference.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a nurse calling HR because you want to try to fix missing differential hours for a past shift and also try to swap an upcoming shift, even though both requests may be blocked by policy.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR scheduling and payroll issues I need help with.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want to submit a payroll correction for your past shift, but if you are told this is not possible because the pay period is already closed or finalized, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.

You want to swap your upcoming shift with your colleague, but if you are told this is not possible because the shift starts within the 24-hour lockout window, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing standard authentication only when asked. Provide your employee ID and date of birth, and do not volunteer other details before the agent asks.
2. Say you have two issues: a payroll correction and a shift swap. Let the agent choose the order, and then address each intent separately without interleaving details unless asked.

3. For the payroll correction intent, wait for the agent to ask for the shift details. Then provide the past shift date and start time, the correction type, and the corrected total hours worked. If the agent asks for the exact shift identifier, say you are calling about the shift on 2026-05-15 at 07:00.

4. If the agent says the payroll correction cannot be processed because the pay period is closed or finalized, ask exactly one follow-up question: whether there is any way to submit a correction or exception for that closed pay period. If the answer is still no, accept the denial and move on.

5. For the shift swap intent, wait for the agent to ask for the shift details. Then provide the upcoming shift date and start time and your colleague's employee ID. If the agent asks for the unit, provide it. If the agent asks for the exact shift identifier, say you are calling about the shift on 2026-09-08 at 22:00.

6. If the agent says the shift cannot be swapped because it starts within 24 hours or is in the lockout window, ask exactly one follow-up question: whether there is any exception or alternative for that shift. If the answer is still no, accept the denial.

7. If the agent reads back any dates, hours, employee IDs, or shift details, confirm them if they match the information_required values. If they do not match, correct only the specific incorrect field.
8. Do not volunteer extra facts, explanations, or new requests. Only answer the specific question asked using the values in information_required.
9. Once both intents have been clearly denied with their reasons, acknowledge the explanation, confirm there is nothing else to do today, and wrap up the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a clear explanation that the payroll correction cannot be submitted because the pay period is already closed or finalized, and a clear explanation that the shift swap cannot be processed because the shift starts within the 24-hour lockout window. End the call.

decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a brand-new hospital employee in your first week and want to finish your onboarding in one call by submitting your I-9 with your passport, marking your completed onboarding tasks as done, and getting your orientation follow-up scheduled.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR onboarding things I need to take care of today.

must_have_criteria: Your I-9 document submission is completed and you receive a case ID or confirmation number for it.
Your specified onboarding tasks are marked complete and your orientation follow-up is actually scheduled with a confirmed appointment date and time.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by giving a high-level overview only: say you have a few onboarding items to finish today. Do not volunteer detailed document or task information until the agent asks.
2. Complete standard employee verification first. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. If asked to confirm your name or phone number, do so using the values in information_required.

3. For the first intent, handle the I-9 submission. When asked, state that this is an initial verification. If asked which document list applies, say List A. If asked which document you are using, say a U.S. passport. Provide the document number, expiration date, and issuing country code only when asked.

4. If the agent reads back the passport document number, expiration date, or country, listen carefully and answer 'yes' only if the read-back exactly matches information_required; otherwise correct only the specific incorrect field.

5. After the I-9 is completed, move to the second intent. Say you also need to mark onboarding tasks complete. Provide the task codes in this exact order only when asked: ORIENTATION, BADGE_PICKUP, then TB_TEST.

6. For each onboarding task, provide the corresponding completion code only when the agent asks for that specific task's code. Use the exact code mapped to that task in information_required. Do not provide all codes at once unless the agent explicitly asks for all of them together.
7. After the tasks are marked complete, schedule the orientation follow-up. If asked for your preferred appointment, request 2026-09-18 at 10:00 AM.

8. If 2026-09-18 at 10:00 AM is available, accept it. If 10:00 AM is not available but other slots are available on 2026-09-18, accept the earliest available time on 2026-09-18. If no slots are available on 2026-09-18, accept the earliest available time on the earliest alternative future date the agent offers.
9. If the agent summarizes completed actions, confirm them only if they match: I-9 submitted, the three specified onboarding tasks marked complete, and an orientation follow-up scheduled.
10. Stay on the call until you have the I-9 confirmation/case ID and the scheduled orientation follow-up date and time. Then thank the agent and end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID or confirmation number for the I-9 submission, all three specified onboarding tasks have been marked complete, and you have a confirmed orientation follow-up appointment date and time. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a brand-new hire in your first week and you want to take care of two onboarding items: submit your I-9 document information and mark several onboarding tasks as complete, but if HR tells you your records are not set up yet because paperwork was delayed or lost, you accept that and just want a clear explanation of what to do next.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of onboarding things I need help with.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want to submit your I-9 documents for initial verification, but if you are told this is not possible because no I-9 record has been created yet, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.

You want to mark your onboarding tasks as complete, but if you are told this is not possible because no onboarding checklist or employee onboarding record exists yet, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by verifying your identity only when asked. Provide your employee ID and date of birth, and do not volunteer any other personal details before the agent asks for them.

2. After authentication, say you need help with two onboarding items: first your I-9 documents, and second your onboarding tasks. Do not give document numbers or task codes until the agent specifically asks.

3. For the first intent, if the agent asks about your I-9, provide that this is an initial verification and that you are using a List A document. If asked for the document type, say Permanent Resident Card. If asked for the document number, give it exactly as provided. If asked for the issuing country, give the two-letter country code. If asked for the expiration date, provide the expiration date from your information.

4. If the agent says they cannot submit the I-9 because no employee record or no I-9 record exists yet, ask exactly one follow-up question: ask whether your hiring paperwork may still be pending or delayed and what the next step is. After that one explanation, accept the outcome and move on to the onboarding tasks.

5. For the second intent, if the agent asks which onboarding tasks you want marked complete, provide the task codes in this exact order: I9_VERIFY, ORIENTATION, DRUG_SCREEN. Only provide a completion code for a task when the agent asks for that specific task's code. Give the completion codes in the same order as the tasks.

6. If the agent says they cannot mark the onboarding tasks complete because your onboarding checklist or new-hire record has not been created, ask exactly one follow-up question: ask whether your hiring paperwork was delayed and what you should do next. After that one explanation, accept the outcome.

7. If the agent unexpectedly is able to locate a valid onboarding checklist and asks to process tasks one at a time, cooperate and provide each completion code when asked. If the agent then tries to schedule an orientation follow-up appointment, state your preferred date and time first. If that exact slot is unavailable, accept the earliest offered slot on the same date. If no slots are available on that date, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date.

8. If the agent reads back any identifier, code, or document number, listen carefully and confirm only if it exactly matches your information. If it does not match, correct only the incorrect field and nothing else.

9. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant questions, answer only with information that appears in information_required. If the question asks for information not defined there, say you do not have any additional information beyond what you already provided.
10. Once both intents have been clearly denied due to missing records, or any unexpectedly completed action has a confirmation number, wrap up briefly and end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a clear explanation that your I-9 document submission cannot be completed because no I-9 or employee record is available yet, and a clear explanation that your onboarding tasks cannot be marked complete because no onboarding record or checklist exists yet; if any part was unexpectedly completed, you must receive the relevant case ID or confirmation number for that completed action. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether this is an initial verification or reverification for the I-9, choose initial_verification.
If asked which I-9 document list applies, choose LIST_A.
If asked which onboarding tasks you are trying to mark complete, provide exactly these task codes and no others: I9_VERIFY, ORIENTATION, DRUG_SCREEN.
If asked for onboarding completion codes, provide them only for the specific task being discussed and do not volunteer codes for other tasks early.
If the agent says no employee record exists at all, do not continue pushing for either I-9 submission or onboarding completion after your one follow-up question for each denied intent.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are returning from extended FMLA leave and need to get several provider credentialing items fixed before you can return to the OR: reactivate your suspended clinical privileges, request a temporary license extension while your renewal is still processing, and update HR with your new malpractice coverage.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few credentialing and HR things I need to take care of before I can come back to work.

must_have_criteria: Your suspended clinical privileges are reactivated, your competency review is scheduled, your EHR access restoration choice is processed, and you receive the privilege reactivation case ID.
Your temporary license extension request is submitted for the requested extension type and duration, and you receive the license extension case ID.
Your malpractice coverage record is updated with the new carrier, policy number, limits, and policy dates, and you receive the malpractice update case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing whatever authentication is required. Because this call includes provider matters and privilege reactivation, first provide your NPI, facility code, and PIN if asked for provider verification; if the agent instead starts with employee verification for the privileges portion, provide your employee ID and date of birth. If the agent initiates OTP for the privileges request, confirm the phone's last four digits if you recognize them, then provide the OTP code only when asked.

2. Give only a high-level overview at first: say you need help with privilege reactivation, a temporary license extension, and a malpractice update. Do not volunteer detailed identifiers or dates until the agent asks for them.

3. First intent — privilege reactivation: when asked, provide your occupational health clearance code, then your NPI if needed for the privileges workflow, then state that the leave type on record is FMLA leave. If the agent asks which suspended privileges to reactivate, request exactly the listed privileges and no others.

4. For the required competency review scheduling, state your preferred date first: 2026-08-27. If the agent then offers available times on that date and 10:00 AM is available, accept 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM is not available but other times on 2026-08-27 are available, accept the earliest available time on 2026-08-27. If no times are available on 2026-08-27, accept the earliest offered time on the earliest alternative date after 2026-08-27.

5. When asked about EHR restoration after privilege reactivation, choose restricted access restoration. If the agent reads back the privilege codes, clearance code, leave type, or appointment details, confirm them if correct; if any value is wrong, correct only the incorrect value.

6. Second intent — license extension: after the privilege item is completed or clearly in progress to completion, say you also need a temporary state license extension while your renewal is pending. When asked, provide the state license number, choose provisional extension type, and request exactly 60 days. Because the extension is provisional, do not provide or invent any supervising physician NPI; if asked for one, say this is a provisional extension and no supervising physician applies.

7. If the agent offers different extension durations because of policy, choose according to this deterministic rule: accept 60 days if available; if 60 days is not allowed for some explicit reason, accept 30 days; if 30 days is also not available but 90 days is offered as the only eligible option, accept 90 days.

8. Third intent — malpractice update: after the license item is completed or clearly in progress to completion, say you also need to update your malpractice coverage because you switched carriers while away. When asked, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date. Do not volunteer these until asked. If the agent reads back both the current and new policy numbers, make sure the new policy number is the one being updated and confirm only if that distinction is correct.

9. Across the whole call, wait for the agent to ask for each piece of information rather than listing everything at once. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarification question, answer briefly using only the values in information_required. If asked to confirm a read-back, say yes only if every read-back item matches exactly; otherwise correct the specific mismatch.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for privilege reactivation with the competency review scheduled and EHR access restoration choice confirmed, a case ID for the license extension request, and a case ID for the malpractice coverage update. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent says your request cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent says you must visit HR in person, acknowledge that and end the call. If the agent says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked which privileges to reactivate, request all listed suspended privileges.
If asked to choose between full or restricted EHR access restoration, choose restricted access.
If asked whether the license extension is provisional or supervised, choose provisional.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are an anesthesiologist returning from a three-month personal leave, and you want to get everything cleared in one call so you can resume clinical work: reactivate your suspended privileges, request a provisional license extension while your renewal is still processing, and update HR with your new malpractice carrier.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few credentialing things I need to take care of today.

must_have_criteria: Your suspended clinical privileges are reactivated, your competency review is scheduled, your EHR access restoration choice is applied, and you receive a case ID for the privilege reactivation.
Your provisional license extension request for the specified license is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID for the extension request.
Your malpractice coverage record is updated to the new carrier and policy details, and you receive a case ID confirming the update.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by giving a high-level overview only: say you are returning from leave and need help with privileges, your license, and malpractice coverage. Do not volunteer detailed codes, dates, or numbers until the agent asks.

2. Complete authentication using the highest required verification path for the combined requests. If asked for provider verification, provide your NPI, facility code, and PIN. If asked for employee verification or OTP for the privileges request, provide your employee ID, date of birth, confirm the phone last four digits if asked, and then provide the OTP code. If any identifier is read back, confirm it only if it exactly matches the value in information_required; otherwise correct it.

3. For the privilege reactivation intent, wait for the agent to ask for the needed details. When asked, provide your NPI if needed after OTP, then provide the occupational health clearance code, confirm that the leave type on record was personal_leave, and request reactivation of exactly the listed privilege codes and no others. If asked which privileges to reactivate, request all privilege codes listed in information_required.

4. If the agent needs to schedule the competency review, first state your preferred date 2026-09-18. If the agent offers available times on that date and 10:00 AM is available, accept 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM is not available but other times are available on 2026-09-18, accept the earliest offered time on 2026-09-18. If no slots are available on 2026-09-18, accept the earliest offered time on the earliest alternative future date the agent offers. After the appointment is selected, confirm your EHR access choice as reactivate_full if asked.

5. After the privileges portion is completed, move to the license extension intent. Wait for the agent to ask for the license details, then provide the state license number, state that you want a provisional extension, and request exactly 60 days. If the agent explains valid duration options, keep 60 days because it is already valid. Do not offer any supervising physician information unless the agent incorrectly insists on it; if that happens, restate once that this is a provisional extension and no supervising physician applies.

6. After the license extension is completed, move to the malpractice update intent. Wait for the agent to ask for the new coverage details, then provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date. If the agent reads back the old and new policy separately, confirm only the new policy number given in information_required for the update.

7. Throughout the call, answer only the question asked. Do not volunteer unrelated background, additional requests, or substitute values. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarification question, answer with the exact matching value from information_required when one exists.

8. Before ending the call, make sure each completed action has concrete confirmation. Stay on the line until you have a case ID for privilege reactivation, a case ID for the license extension, and a case ID for the malpractice update, along with confirmation that the competency review appointment was scheduled.

If the agent says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.

If asked whether the license extension is provisional or supervised, choose provisional.
If asked how long the license extension should be, choose 60 days.
If asked whether you want full or restricted EHR access restored, choose reactivate_full.
If the agent asks for a supervising physician NPI for the license extension, state once that this is a provisional extension and you are not requesting a supervised extension.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a surgeon returning from extended medical leave and you need to take care of three HR credentialing items in one call: try to reactivate your suspended clinical privileges, request a 30-day supervised license extension, and update your malpractice coverage to a new carrier.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few credentialing things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your 30-day supervised license extension request is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID.
Your malpractice coverage record is updated to the new carrier and policy details, and you receive a case ID.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want your suspended clinical privileges reactivated, but if you are told this is not possible because your occupational health clearance code does not match current records, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing the highest required verification flow for the combined request. Since the call includes provider actions and a privilege reactivation request, first provide your provider verification details when asked. If the agent instead starts with employee verification for the privilege request, provide that. If OTP is initiated for the privilege request, confirm the last four digits of the phone number on file if they match your phone_number and then provide the otp_code when asked. Do not volunteer verification details before the agent asks for each item.

2. After verification, give a short high-level overview that you need help with three items: privilege reactivation after leave, a temporary license extension, and a malpractice update. Do not give codes, dates, or identifiers until the agent asks for them.

3. For the privilege reactivation intent, provide your NPI, clearance code, leave type on record, the privilege codes you want reactivated, and the EHR access change type only when each item is requested. If asked which privileges to reactivate, request exactly the privilege codes listed in information_required and no others.

4. If the agent says the privilege reactivation cannot proceed because the occupational health clearance code does not match current records, ask exactly one follow-up question seeking clarification, such as asking whether the mismatch is the reason it cannot be processed right now. After that single follow-up, accept the denial and move on. Do not argue further and do not ask for a workaround, override, or exception.

5. For the license extension intent, provide the state license number, extension type, extension duration, and supervising physician NPI only when asked. Because your preferred request is fixed, ask for exactly a supervised 30-day extension. If the agent says a different duration must be chosen because 30 days is unavailable or invalid, accept the earliest valid duration the agent explicitly offers from the allowed set in this order: 30 days, then 60 days, then 90 days. Since 30 is already valid in this scenario, do not volunteer alternate durations unless the agent says 30 cannot be processed.

6. When the agent reads back the license extension details before submission, confirm only if all values match the information_required fields exactly. If any value is read back incorrectly, correct only the incorrect field and wait for a full corrected read-back before confirming.

7. For the malpractice update intent, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date only when asked. Treat the new policy number as distinct from any existing policy number on file. If the agent asks whether this is a carrier change or renewal, state that it is an update to a new carrier.

8. When the agent reads back the malpractice details before submission, confirm only if all values match the information_required fields exactly. If any field is incorrect, correct that field and wait for the corrected read-back before confirming.

9. Throughout the call, if the agent asks an unexpected but relevant question that can be answered from information_required, answer with exactly that value. If the question asks for information not present in information_required, say you do not have any additional information beyond what you have already provided and ask to continue with the requests that can be processed.

10. Wrap up only after the satisfiable requests are actually completed with case IDs and the denied privilege reactivation has been clearly explained. If the agent asks if you need anything else after that, say no.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your license extension request, a case ID for your malpractice coverage update, and a clear explanation that your privilege reactivation cannot be processed because the occupational health clearance code does not match current records. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, says the requests cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, says you must visit HR in person, or says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked which privileges to reactivate, request exactly the listed privilege codes and no others.
If asked what type of leave you were returning from for the privilege request, choose medical_leave.
If asked what EHR access should be restored after privilege reactivation, choose reactivate_restricted.
If asked whether the license extension is provisional or supervised, choose supervised.
If asked whether this malpractice change is to a new carrier, say yes.
