user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are returning from medical leave and transferring to a new facility in Florida, and you need your suspended clinical privileges reactivated, your DEA registration moved to the new site, and your malpractice coverage updated so all three prerequisites are completed before you can start there.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few credentialing and HR items I need help with before I start at a new facility.

must_have_criteria: Your suspended clinical privileges are reactivated, your competency review appointment is scheduled, your EHR access is restored as full access, and you receive a privilege reactivation case ID.
Your DEA registration transfer to the new facility in Florida is submitted with the correct effective date and you receive a DEA transfer case ID.
Your malpractice coverage is updated to the new carrier and policy for the new state with the stated coverage limits and effective dates, and you receive a malpractice update case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing whatever verification is required. Because your requests include provider actions and OTP-protected actions, first provide your NPI, home facility code, and PIN when asked. If the agent then initiates OTP, confirm the phone by giving the last four digits of your phone number only if asked, and then provide the OTP code when asked. Do not volunteer identifiers before the agent asks for them.

2. After verification, give a brief high-level overview that you are returning from leave and transferring to a new facility in another state, and that you need help with privilege reactivation, DEA transfer, and malpractice coverage update. Do not provide detailed codes, dates, or numbers until the agent asks for each one.

3. For the privilege reactivation intent, wait for the agent to retrieve and review your provider and privilege records. When asked, provide your occupational health clearance code, confirm that your leave type on record was medical_leave, and confirm that you want the listed suspended privileges reactivated. If asked which privileges to reactivate, request exactly the privilege codes listed in your information_required and no others.

4. For the competency review appointment required for privilege reactivation, state your preferred date as 2026-08-28 and your preferred time as 10:00 AM only when the agent asks. If 10:00 AM on 2026-08-28 is available, accept it. If 10:00 AM on 2026-08-28 is not available but other slots on 2026-08-28 are offered, accept the earliest available time on 2026-08-28. If no slots are available on 2026-08-28, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future alternative date returned by the agent.

5. Before the privilege reactivation is submitted, listen to the agent's read-back and confirm only if the NPI, clearance code, leave type, and privilege codes match exactly. After reactivation, if asked what EHR access to restore, choose reactivate_full. Stay on the call until you receive the privilege reactivation case ID.

6. After the privilege reactivation portion is complete, move to the DEA transfer intent. Wait for the agent to review the current DEA record first. When asked, provide the DEA number, the new facility code, the new state code FL, the transfer reason role_change, and the effective date 2026-09-10. If the agent says the new state as a full name, accept that as long as it corresponds to FL.

7. Before the DEA transfer is submitted, listen to the read-back carefully and confirm only if the DEA number, new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, and effective date all match exactly. Stay on the call until you receive the DEA transfer case ID.

8. After the DEA transfer portion is complete, move to the malpractice coverage update intent. Wait for the agent to review the current malpractice record first. When asked, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date 2026-09-01, and expiration date 2027-09-01. Do not confuse the new policy number with any existing policy number the agent may mention from the current record.

9. Before the malpractice update is submitted, confirm only if the carrier, new policy number, coverage limits, effective date, and expiration date are read back exactly as provided. Stay on the call until you receive the malpractice update case ID.

10. At the end of the call, confirm that all three items were completed and that you have all three case IDs. If the agent summarizes anything incorrectly, correct only the specific wrong field and wait for a corrected read-back.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for the privilege reactivation with competency review scheduled and full EHR access restoration confirmed, a case ID for the DEA registration transfer to the new facility in Florida, and a case ID for the malpractice coverage update to the new carrier and policy. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked which privileges to reactivate, request exactly the listed suspended privilege codes and no others.
If asked what type of EHR access to restore after privilege reactivation, choose reactivate_full.
If asked for the leave type associated with the privilege suspension, choose medical_leave.
If asked for the DEA transfer reason, choose role_change.
If asked for the new state in full-name form instead of abbreviation, state Florida, which corresponds to FL.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are returning from medical leave and moving into a new physician assistant role at another hospital campus in Colorado, so you need your suspended clinical privileges reactivated, your DEA registration transferred to the new facility, and your malpractice coverage updated for the new state before your new start date.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR credentialing things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your suspended clinical privileges are reactivated for the privilege codes you confirm, your competency review is scheduled, your EHR access is restored as full access, and you receive a privilege reactivation case ID.
Your DEA registration transfer to the new facility and new state is submitted with the requested effective date, and you receive a DEA transfer case ID.
Your malpractice coverage is updated to the new carrier, policy number, coverage limits, and policy dates for the new state, and you receive a malpractice update case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing whatever identity verification the agent requires. Because this call includes provider-sensitive and OTP-required actions, first provide your NPI, home facility code, and 4-digit PIN when asked. If the agent then initiates OTP, confirm the last four digits of the phone number on file only if they match what the agent says, and then provide the 6-digit OTP code when asked. Do not volunteer any credential before the agent asks for it.

2. After verification, give a high-level overview only: say you need help with privilege reactivation, a DEA transfer, and a malpractice update because you are returning from medical leave and transferring to a different-state campus. Do not provide detailed codes, dates, or numbers until requested.

3. First intent — privilege reactivation: when the agent asks, provide your employee ID and date of birth if needed for this flow, then provide your NPI if asked again for the privilege record lookup, your occupational health clearance code, and confirm that your leave type on record is medical_leave. When asked which suspended privileges you want reactivated, request exactly the listed privileges and no others.

4. For the required competency review appointment, state your preferred date as 2026-07-08 and your preferred time as 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM on 2026-07-08 is available, accept it. If 10:00 AM is not available but other slots are available on 2026-07-08, accept the earliest available time on 2026-07-08. If no slots are available on 2026-07-08, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date. After the agent reads back the privilege selections, leave type, clearance code, appointment, and EHR access choice, confirm if the read-back matches exactly. If asked what EHR access to restore, choose reactivate_full.

5. Second intent — DEA transfer: once the privilege request is completed or the agent moves to the next item, provide the DEA number when asked, then provide the new facility code, the new state code as CO, and the transfer reason as role_change. If the agent asks for the effective date, give 2026-07-20. If the agent reads back the DEA number, new facility, state, reason, or effective date, confirm only if they match exactly.

6. Third intent — malpractice update: when the agent asks for the new malpractice information, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date. If the agent reads back the carrier, policy number, limits, or dates, confirm only if they match exactly. If asked whether this is a new policy for the new state, say yes.

7. Across the call, do not volunteer more detail than the current step requires. Answer unexpected but relevant clarification questions with only the exact value from information_required. If the agent asks you to choose among multiple valid options not already covered here, choose the earliest date and, on the same date, the earliest time.

8. Before the call ends, remain on the line until each completed request has a concrete confirmation such as a case ID and the scheduled competency review has a confirmed date and time. If the agent gives a summary, verify each item carefully and confirm only if all details match.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a privilege reactivation case ID with your competency review appointment confirmed and full EHR access restoration confirmed, a DEA transfer case ID for the new facility and state, and a malpractice update case ID for the new policy details. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent says your request cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent says you must visit HR in person, acknowledge that and end the call. If the agent says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked which privileges to reactivate, request exactly the two listed privilege codes and no others.
If asked to choose between full or restricted EHR restoration, choose full access.
If the agent states the new state as Colorado instead of CO, treat that as the same state and confirm it.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are relocating to a new facility in Texas and need HR to handle three credentialing tasks in one call: transfer your DEA registration to the new facility, update your malpractice coverage for the new state, and submit a provisional license extension so you can keep practicing while your new-state renewal is still being processed.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few provider credentialing things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your DEA transfer to the new facility is completed and you receive a case ID or other explicit transfer confirmation.
Your malpractice coverage update for the new state is completed and you receive a case ID or other explicit update confirmation.
Your provisional license extension request for 60 days is submitted and you receive a case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by saying you have a few provider credentialing items to take care of, then wait for the agent to drive verification. Do not volunteer detailed identifiers until asked.

2. For provider verification, provide your NPI, home facility code, and 4-digit PIN only when requested. If the agent initiates OTP because of the DEA transfer, confirm the phone number on file only if you recognize the last four digits; then provide the 6-digit OTP code when asked.

3. Handle the DEA transfer first if the agent is ready for it. Provide your DEA number, then provide the new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, and effective date only when each is requested. If the agent asks you to confirm the transfer details, say yes after the read-back matches your information.

4. After the DEA transfer is completed, move to the malpractice update. Provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date only when asked. If the agent reads back the old and new policy information, confirm only if the new policy number matches your information exactly and the coverage amounts are correct.

5. After the malpractice update is completed, move to the license extension request. Provide the state license number only when asked. If the agent asks what type of extension you need, choose provisional. If the agent asks for duration, choose 60 days. If the agent asks for a supervising physician, say that none applies because this is a provisional extension.

6. For any read-back of IDs, codes, dates, or dollar amounts, listen carefully and confirm only if every value matches exactly. If a value is read back incorrectly, correct only the specific incorrect field and nothing else.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You accepted a role change that requires relocating to a different campus in another state, and you need HR to handle the related credentialing updates: transfer your DEA registration to the new facility, update your malpractice coverage for the destination state, and submit a provisional license extension so you can keep practicing while your new-state license application is still pending.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few provider credentialing updates I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your DEA registration transfer to the new facility is completed and you receive a case ID or confirmation tied to the transfer.
Your malpractice coverage update for the new state is completed with the new carrier and policy details recorded, and you receive a case ID or confirmation for the update.
Your provisional license extension request is submitted for the specified license and duration, and you receive a case ID for the extension request.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing provider verification when asked. Provide your NPI, home facility code, and PIN only when requested. Because DEA transfer is part of the call, complete OTP verification as well when prompted. If asked to confirm the phone number on file by last four digits, confirm it only if it matches the phone_number in information_required; if it does not match, say you do not recognize it and accept being told to visit HR in person.

2. After verification is complete, give a brief overview that you need three things handled in this order: DEA transfer, malpractice update, and license extension. Do not volunteer specific identifiers or dates until the agent asks for them.

3. First intent — DEA transfer: when asked, provide your DEA number, then your new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, and transfer effective date. If the agent asks for the new state in full-name form, still provide the two-letter code from information_required. If the agent reads back the DEA number, facility code, state code, or effective date, confirm only if it exactly matches the values in information_required. If the agent asks for confirmation before submission, say yes.

4. After the DEA transfer is completed, stay on the call until you are given a concrete transfer case ID or confirmation and told the relevant state monitoring program notification has been sent or completed.

5. Second intent — malpractice update: when asked, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date. Do not provide the old policy information unless the agent specifically asks you to confirm what is currently on file. If the agent reads back the coverage values or policy number, confirm only if they exactly match information_required. If asked to confirm before submission, say yes.
6. After the malpractice update is completed, stay on the call until you receive a concrete case ID or confirmation for the coverage update.

7. Third intent — license extension: when asked, provide the state license number, extension type, and extension days. Because the extension type is provisional, if the agent asks for a supervising physician NPI, say that you are requesting a provisional extension and do not have a supervising physician for this request. If the agent offers different extension durations, accept only the exact duration in information_required. If the agent reads back the license number, extension type, or duration, confirm only if they exactly match information_required. If asked to confirm before submission, say yes.
8. After the license extension request is completed, stay on the call until you receive a concrete case ID for the extension request and understand that it has been submitted for review.

9. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions that are directly necessary to complete one of the three defined requests, answer only with the exact corresponding value from information_required. If the question asks for information not present there and not required by the applicable policy, say you do not have anything else to add and ask to continue with the request.
10. At the end, briefly confirm the three outcomes in order and end the call once all three have either been completed with confirmation IDs or a failure condition has been reached.

user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR things I need help with related to my new baby.

must_have_criteria: Your newborn is added to your visa petition as a dependent and you receive the amendment ID or equivalent confirmation identifier.

negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin with authentication only. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked, then complete OTP steps only when the agent requests them. Do not volunteer any detailed case information before verification is complete.

2. When the OTP is initiated, first confirm the last four digits of the phone number if they match the phone number in information_required. Then provide the OTP code exactly as listed in information_required. If the agent says the code failed, retry the same code one time only.

3. After authentication is complete and the agent asks what you need, give a high-level overview that you need help with a visa dependent addition, bonding FMLA, and a short PTO request before the leave. Do not provide details for any one item until asked.

4. Handle the visa dependent addition first. When asked, provide the visa petition number. If asked to confirm or read back the petition number, confirm it exactly. Then provide the dependent's first and last name and spell each name letter by letter. Provide the relationship, dependent date of birth, country of birth, and USCIS receipt number only when each is requested. If the agent reads back any identifier or spelling, confirm it exactly if correct.

5. For the FMLA request second, provide the leave category as bonding when asked. Provide the leave start date as 2026-08-24 and leave end date as 2026-09-18 only when requested. If 2026-09-21 at 10:00 AM is available, accept it. If that time is not available but the same date has other times, accept the earliest offered time on 2026-09-21. If no slots are available on 2026-09-21, accept the earliest offered time on the nearest future date that is on or after 2026-09-21.

6. For the PTO request third, provide the PTO type as pto when asked. Request PTO from 2026-08-19 through 2026-08-21. Wait for the agent to tell you which dates count and how many PTO days will be deducted. If the agent says all requested dates are eligible, confirm the request exactly as read back. If any requested date is ineligible but the agent offers alternatives, choose the earliest eligible date range that preserves the same number of PTO days and ends before 2026-08-24. If multiple alternative ranges satisfy that rule, choose the earliest starting range. If no eligible pre-FMLA range is available, ask one follow-up question about whether any shorter range before 2026-08-24 is available; then accept the best available option that ends before 2026-08-24.

7. Before each submission, if the agent summarizes dates, names, or identifiers, listen and confirm only if the read-back matches the information_required values exactly. If something is wrong, correct only the specific wrong field and nothing else.

8. At the end, stay on the call until you have a concrete confirmation for each completed request: an amendment or confirmation ID for the visa dependent addition, a case ID for FMLA plus the scheduled return-to-work check-in details, and a case ID for the PTO request with the deducted dates confirmed.

negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by giving a high-level overview only: say you have three items related to a recent adoption. Do not volunteer detailed dates, IDs, or names until the agent asks.

2. Complete authentication exactly as requested. For standard verification, provide your employee ID and date of birth. If OTP is initiated, first confirm whether the last four digits of the phone number on file match your phone number ending in 4826, then provide the OTP code only when asked.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You were recently diagnosed with a condition that requires surgery and recovery time. You need to file FMLA for the surgery and recovery period, swap an upcoming shift that conflicts with a pre-op appointment, and request a couple of sick days right before the procedure so you can rest.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your FMLA leave is opened successfully for your medical condition, a case ID is provided, and your return-to-work check-in is scheduled with the appointment details confirmed.
Your upcoming shift swap is confirmed with your colleague and you receive a case ID for the swap.
Your sick leave request for the days before FMLA starts is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID with the deducted dates confirmed.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing authentication only when asked. Provide your employee ID and date of birth for standard verification. If OTP is initiated for the leave request, confirm whether the last four digits of the phone number match your phone number on file, then provide the OTP code only when asked. Do not volunteer other details before the agent asks.

2. After authentication, say you have three needs: FMLA for surgery and recovery, a shift swap for an upcoming pre-op conflict, and a sick leave request before the procedure. If the agent wants to handle them one at a time, follow the agent's order without resistance.

3. For the FMLA request, provide details only when asked: state that the leave category is for your own medical condition, give the leave start date and leave end date, and confirm any read-back before submission. If the agent asks for a preferred return-to-work check-in date and time, first request 2026-10-06 at 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM on 2026-10-06 is unavailable, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-10-06. If no slots are available on 2026-10-06, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date on or after 2026-10-06.

4. For the shift swap, provide the upcoming shift details only when asked, then provide your colleague's employee ID only when asked. If the agent reads back the shift date, time, unit, or colleague ID, confirm if it matches. If the swap is eligible and your colleague is certified, explicitly confirm that you want the swap submitted.

5. For the sick leave request, provide the requested start and end dates only when asked, and specify that the leave type should be sick leave. If the agent explains how many days will be deducted, accept the calculation if it matches the requested dates that count. If the agent asks you to confirm before submission, say yes.

6. Across the whole conversation, do not volunteer extra medical details beyond saying it is surgery and recovery for your own medical condition unless the agent asks. Answer direct questions briefly and exactly with the values in information_required.
7. If the agent reads back any identifier, code, date, or appointment details, confirm it if correct and correct it only if it does not exactly match the values in information_required.

8. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant question that is not covered elsewhere, answer only if the answer is directly available in information_required. If it is not available there, say you do not have additional details and ask to proceed with the information already provided.

9. Before ending the call, make sure each completed request has concrete confirmation: a case ID for FMLA, a scheduled return-to-work check-in with date and time, a case ID for the shift swap, and a case ID for the sick leave request.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your FMLA leave, your return-to-work check-in has been scheduled with confirmed date and time, you have received a case ID for your shift swap, and you have received a case ID for your sick leave request with the dates confirmed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

You need to file FMLA for the surgery and recovery period, swap an upcoming shift that conflicts with your pre-op plans, and also ask for sick leave for a couple of days before the FMLA starts even though that request may be denied because your sick balance is too low.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR things I need help with related to an upcoming surgery.

must_have_criteria: Your FMLA leave for your own medical condition is successfully opened for the surgery and recovery period, and you receive the FMLA case ID.
Your upcoming shift swap is confirmed with your colleague, and you receive the shift swap case ID.
nice_to_have_criteria: You request sick leave for the days before your FMLA starts, but if you are told this is not possible because you do not have enough sick leave balance, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing authentication only when asked. Provide your employee ID and date of birth first. Because leave is part of the call, if the agent initiates OTP, confirm the phone number on file if it matches the last four digits of your phone number and then provide the OTP code when asked. Do not volunteer any other details before the agent asks.
2. After authentication, give a brief overview that you need help with FMLA for surgery, a shift swap, and sick leave before the surgery. Do not provide dates or IDs until the agent asks for them.

3. First intent — FMLA: when asked for leave details, provide that the leave category is for your own medical condition, with leave start date 2026-08-10 and leave end date 2026-09-21. If asked why, say it is for surgery and recovery. If asked to confirm the details before submission, say yes.

4. If the agent schedules the required return-to-work check-in, state your preferred date and time as 2026-09-21 at 10:00 AM. If that exact slot is available, accept it. If that date is available but 10:00 AM is not, accept the earliest offered time on 2026-09-21. If no slots are available on 2026-09-21, accept the earliest offered time on the nearest future date on or after 2026-09-22. Do not choose based on convenience.

5. Second intent — shift swap: after the FMLA portion is completed or clearly in progress, ask to handle the shift swap. When asked, provide the shift date as 2026-08-02, the shift time as 11:00 PM. Provide your colleague's employee ID only when asked. If the agent reads back the shift or colleague information, confirm it if it matches exactly.

6. For the shift swap, if the agent confirms your colleague is eligible and summarizes the swap before submission, explicitly confirm that you want to proceed. Stay on the call until you receive the shift swap case ID.

7. Third intent — sick leave request: after the shift swap is handled, ask to request sick leave for 2026-08-06 through 2026-08-07 using sick leave. Do not volunteer a reason unless asked; if asked, say it is for pre-op rest before surgery.

8. If the agent says the sick leave request cannot be approved because you do not have enough sick leave balance, ask exactly one follow-up question to confirm whether there is any way to use the remaining sick balance for those dates. After the answer, accept the denial and do not argue further.

9. If the agent offers to shorten the sick leave request because of the low balance, accept only if the agent explicitly says at least one of the requested dates can be approved using your remaining sick leave. In that case, choose the earliest single date the agent says is approvable. If no requested date is approvable, accept the denial. Do not propose alternate dates yourself.

10. Before ending the call, make sure you have the FMLA case ID, the shift swap case ID, and a clear final answer on the sick leave request. If the agent only promises to process something later without a case ID or confirmed denial outcome, continue asking for the final result.

11. For any unexpected question that is necessary to continue, answer briefly and only with information contained in information_required. If asked for something not defined there, say you do not have any additional information to provide.
12. If the agent reads back any identifier, date, or code, confirm only if it matches exactly; otherwise correct the specific item and nothing else.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your FMLA leave, a case ID for your shift swap, and a clear explanation of the outcome of your sick leave request, including denial if there is not enough balance. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.

If the agent asks for the shift using date and time instead of shift ID, use the date 2026-08-02 and time 11:00 PM; provide the shift ID only if asked for it explicitly.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a new hospital employee in your first week and want to get several HR tasks done in one call: finish your onboarding checklist and book the required orientation follow-up, submit your initial I-9 document information for work authorization, and request a couple of PTO days for a family event you planned before starting.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR things I need to take care of today.

must_have_criteria: Your specified onboarding tasks are marked complete and your orientation follow-up appointment is scheduled with a confirmed appointment date and time.
Your initial I-9 verification is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID.
Your PTO request for the requested family-event dates is submitted successfully with the deducted days confirmed and you receive a case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing identity verification only when asked. Provide your employee ID and date of birth, and do not volunteer other details before the agent asks.

2. After verification, say you have three things to handle: onboarding completion with orientation follow-up, I-9 submission, and a PTO request. Do not give detailed values until the agent asks for each one.

3. First intent — onboarding: when asked which onboarding tasks you completed, provide the two task codes in this order: OCC_HEALTH, then HIPAA_TRAIN. When asked for completion codes, provide the completion code for OCC_HEALTH first, then the completion code for HIPAA_TRAIN.

4. For orientation scheduling, state your preferred date is 2026-08-18 and your preferred time is 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM on 2026-08-18 is available, accept it. If 10:00 AM is not available but other times are available on 2026-08-18, accept the earliest offered time on 2026-08-18. If no slots are available on 2026-08-18, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date.

5. Second intent — I-9: when asked, state this is an initial verification. If asked which document list applies, choose LIST_A. If asked for the document type, choose EAD. Provide the document number exactly as listed in information_required. Provide the expiration date and issuing country code only when asked. If the agent reads back the document number, confirm it only if it matches exactly.

6. Third intent — PTO: when asked for the dates, request PTO for 2026-08-20 through 2026-08-21. If asked whether this is PTO or sick leave, choose PTO. If asked for the reason, say it is for a pre-planned family event. Wait for the agent to tell you the exact deducted days and counted dates before confirming.

7. If the agent says any requested PTO date is not eligible and offers alternatives, accept the earliest offered start date that is after 2026-08-21, and use the two-day range starting on that accepted date. If the agent instead offers multiple same-length alternative ranges, choose the range with the earliest start date. If only single dates are possible, choose the earliest two eligible dates the agent offers in chronological order.

8. For any read-back of names, codes, dates, or document numbers, respond with a simple confirmation if correct. If any item is incorrect, correct only the incorrect item and do not add extra information.

9. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarification questions, answer using only the values in information_required. Do not invent missing details. If asked something not covered by the defined information, say you do not have any additional information beyond what you already provided.

10. Before ending the call, make sure all three intents have been addressed: onboarding tasks completed and orientation scheduled, I-9 submitted with a case ID, and PTO submitted with deducted days and a case ID.

End the call.

decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing identity verification first. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. Do not volunteer other details before the agent asks. If the agent asks what you need help with, say you have onboarding, I-9, and PTO questions.

4. After the onboarding portion is resolved, move to the I-9 request. Say you also need to submit your I-9 documents. Wait for the agent to ask for the document details before providing them. 6. After the I-9 is submitted and you have the case ID, move to the PTO request. Wait for the agent to ask for the dates and PTO type. Request PTO from 2026-10-08 through 2026-10-09 for a family event, and specify the PTO type as pto if asked to choose between PTO and sick leave.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a nurse doing end-of-week schedule cleanup and need to fix incorrectly recorded on-call hours from a recent shift, register yourself as available for on-call coverage for the next two weeks, and swap an upcoming shift with a colleague because of a scheduling conflict.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR scheduling and payroll things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your payroll correction for the on-call hours is submitted for the correct shift and you receive a payroll correction case ID.
Your on-call availability for the full two-week window is registered for the correct unit and tier, and you receive a registration ID.
Your shift swap with your colleague is confirmed for the correct upcoming shift and you receive a shift swap case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing identity verification only when asked. Provide your employee ID and date of birth, and do not volunteer any other details until the agent asks.

2. If the agent asks what you need help with after verification, say you have three items: a payroll correction for on-call hours, an on-call availability registration, and a shift swap. Do not provide the detailed dates, hours, or colleague information until asked for each item.

3. First intent — payroll correction: when asked for the shift details, provide the payroll correction shift date and start time. When asked what is wrong, say the on-call hours were recorded incorrectly. When asked for the correction type, say on_call_hours. When asked for the corrected hours, provide 8.0 as the correct total hours for that shift. If the agent reads back the shift, correction type, or hours, confirm only if they exactly match your information_required.

4. For the payroll correction, if the agent says the correction is eligible and asks to proceed, explicitly confirm. Stay on the call until the correction has actually been submitted and a case ID has been given. A statement that it will be handled later is not enough.

5. Second intent — on-call registration: after the payroll correction is completed or clearly finished, say you also need to register on-call availability. Provide the unit code only when asked. Provide the availability start date and end date only when asked. When asked for tier, choose primary. When asked about blackout dates, say there are no blackout dates, so the list should be empty. If the agent reads back the unit, window, tier, or blackout dates, confirm only if they exactly match your information_required.

6. For the on-call registration, if the agent offers to register the availability as stated, confirm. Stay on the call until the registration is actually completed and a registration ID has been provided.

7. Third intent — shift swap: after the on-call registration is completed or clearly finished, say you need to swap an upcoming shift with a colleague. Provide the shift date and start time when asked. Provide the colleague employee ID only when asked. Do not guess or provide certification details; let the agent verify those. If the agent reads back the shift and colleague information, confirm only if it exactly matches your information_required.

8. For the shift swap, if the agent says the shift is eligible and the colleague is qualified, explicitly confirm the swap. Stay on the call until the swap is actually confirmed and a case ID has been provided.
9. If the agent asks you to choose the order of the three requests, always handle them in this fixed order: payroll correction first, on-call registration second, shift swap third.

10. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarifying question and the answer is directly available in information_required, provide that exact value. If the answer is not defined in information_required, say you only have the details already provided and do not invent anything.
11. If the agent summarizes completed actions at the end, listen carefully and confirm only if all three outcomes and all IDs are present and correspond to the correct requests.
12. When all three requests have been completed with their corresponding IDs, thank the agent briefly and end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a payroll correction case ID for the on-call hours correction, a registration ID for the on-call availability window, and a shift swap case ID for the upcoming shift with your colleague. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, says you must visit HR in person, or says your request cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether there are any blackout dates within the on-call availability window, say there are none.
If asked which on-call tier you want, choose primary.
If asked for the payroll correction hours, provide the correct total hours worked for the shift, not a difference.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You have three HR tasks to handle in one call: correct missing on-call hours for a past shift, register your future on-call availability, and try to swap an upcoming shift, even though that swap is likely to be denied because it starts within 24 hours.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your payroll correction for the past shift's on-call hours is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID.
Your on-call availability registration is completed for your requested future window and you receive a registration ID.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want to swap your upcoming shift with your colleague, but if you are told it is not possible because the shift starts within the 24-hour lockout window, ask one follow-up question and then accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing authentication only when asked. Provide your employee ID and date of birth, and do not volunteer other details until the agent asks.

2. After authentication, say you have three things to handle: a payroll correction, on-call registration, and a shift swap. Do not give the specific dates, hours, unit, or colleague information until the agent asks for each item.

3. For the payroll correction intent, provide the past shift date and time when asked: 2026-06-20 at 7:00 PM. If the agent asks for the correction type, say on-call hours. If the agent asks for the correct total hours, say 12.5 hours.

4. If the agent reads back the payroll correction details, confirm them only if they match exactly. Stay on the call until the payroll correction is actually submitted and a case ID is given. A promise to submit it later is not enough.

5. For the on-call registration intent, provide the unit code only when asked: 4A-MED. If asked for the availability window, give 2026-08-18 through 2026-08-31. If asked for the tier, say primary. If asked about blackout dates, say there are none and use an empty blackout list.

6. If the agent offers to review any existing on-call registrations or asks you to confirm the new registration details, confirm only the exact window 2026-08-18 to 2026-08-31, unit 4A-MED, primary tier, and no blackout dates. Stay on the call until the registration is completed and a registration ID is given.
7. For the shift swap intent, provide the upcoming shift date and time only when asked: 2026-08-10 at 10:00 PM. If asked for the colleague's employee ID, provide EMP676713.

8. If the agent says the shift cannot be swapped because it starts within 24 hours or is in the lockout window, ask exactly one follow-up question asking whether there is any exception or workaround for that policy. If the agent still denies it, accept the denial and move on without arguing further.

9. If the agent instead asks to confirm swap details before checking eligibility, only confirm the same shift date and time and the same colleague employee ID. Do not propose a different shift, different colleague, or alternative scheduling request.
10. For any read-back of IDs, dates, times, hours, or codes across the whole call, confirm only if the information matches exactly; otherwise correct the specific wrong item and nothing else.

11. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions that are directly tied to one of the three defined requests, answer using only the values in information_required. If the question asks for information not provided there, say you do not have anything else to add.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for the payroll correction, a registration ID for the on-call availability registration, and a clear explanation that the shift swap cannot be completed because the shift starts within the 24-hour lockout window. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether there are any blackout dates within the on-call availability window, say no and use an empty list.
If asked which correction type applies to the payroll issue, choose on_call_hours.
If the agent asks for the shift by identifier after first asking for the date and time, provide the corresponding shift ID from information_required for the relevant request.

user_config.name: Keiko Mori
user_config.user_persona: You're direct and to the point—you don't have time for lengthy explanations or unnecessary back-and-forth. You speak curtly, getting straight to what you need without much small talk or pleasantries. You want the system to be fast and efficient, and you'll show your frustration if things move slowly or require extra steps.

scenario_context.premise: A nurse wants to correct missing on-call hours, register for on-call availability, and swap an upcoming shift. The payroll correction and on-call registration are straightforward, but the shift they want to swap is within the 24-hour lockout window.
